mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in housing units sold (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day forward return in periods without a 5% increase in HOUST (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over the next 60 trading days signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 60-day forward return in periods without a 10% decline in INDPRO (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 20 trading days signals a weakening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day forward return in periods without a 0.5% increase in UNRATE (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in housing units sold (HOUST) and a 10% increase in real personal income (RSAFS) over the next 5 trading days signals a strengthening demand for housing and a corresponding increase in consumer spending, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 5% and RSAFS > 10% in 5 trading days
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day forward return in periods without a 5% increase in HOUST and a 10% increase in RSAFS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the next 20 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day forward return in periods without a 1% increase in FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 10% decline in real personal income (RSAFS) over the next 60 trading days signals a weakening supply chain and a corresponding decrease in consumer spending, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > -5% and RSAFS > -10% in 60 trading days
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 60-day forward return in periods without a 5% decline in INDPRO and a 10% decline in RSAFS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 20 trading days signals a strengthening labor market, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day forward return in periods without a 0.5% decrease in UNRATE (t-test p > 0.05).
